text.title: Trade-off Decision Methods Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Every decision routed to ATAM; team burns weeks on routine 2-option choices.
antipattern.root-cause: r1 violated; ATAM treated as default.
antipattern.fix: Use matrix by default; reserve ATAM for multi-attribute conflicts.

antipattern.symptom: Matrix scores a perf-vs-security conflict the team had to relitigate; ATAM would have surfaced the trade-off.
antipattern.fix: Detect multi-attribute conflicts (>2 conflicting NFRs); escalate to ATAM.

antipattern.symptom: Regulatory-deadline decision ranked by matrix; cheaper option chosen, deadline missed.
antipattern.fix: Compute $/week of delay; rerun the decision with CoD as a criterion.

antipattern.symptom: High-uncertainty bet committed to today; cheaper to wait for evaluation but option value never modelled.
antipattern.fix: Compare commit-now value vs option-of-waiting before signing.
